member.name: T:FixedMathSharp.BoundingArea
member.summary: Represents a lightweight, axis-aligned bounding area with fixed-point precision, optimized for 2D or simplified 3D use cases.
member.remarks:
The BoundingArea is designed for performance-critical scenarios where only a minimal bounding volume is required. It offers fast containment and intersection checks with other bounds but lacks the full feature set of BoundingBox.
Use Cases: - Efficient spatial queries in 2D or constrained 3D spaces (e.g., terrain maps or collision grids). - Simplified bounding volume checks where rotation or complex shape fitting is not needed. - Can be used as a broad-phase bounding volume to cull objects before more precise checks with BoundingBox or BoundingSphere.

member.name: T:FixedMathSharp.BoundingBox
member.summary: Represents an axis-aligned bounding box with fixed-point precision, capable of encapsulating 3D objects for more complex spatial checks.
member.remarks:
The BoundingBox provides a more detailed representation of an object's spatial extent compared to BoundingArea. It is useful in 3D scenarios requiring more precise volume fitting and supports a wider range of operations, including more complex intersection checks.
Use Cases: - Encapsulating objects in 3D space for collision detection, ray intersection, or visibility testing. - Used in physics engines, rendering pipelines, and 3D simulations to represent object boundaries. - Supports more precise intersection tests than BoundingArea, making it ideal for detailed spatial queries.

member.name: T:FixedMathSharp.BoundingBox.BoundingBoxState
member.summary: Represents the state of a three-dimensional axis-aligned bounding box using its minimum and maximum coordinates.
member.remarks: The bounding box is defined by two points: the minimum and maximum corners in 3D space. This structure is immutable and can be used to describe spatial boundaries for geometric computations, collision detection, or spatial queries.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.BoundingBox.DistanceToSurface(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Calculates the shortest distance from a given point to the surface of the bounding box. If the point lies inside the box, the distance is zero.
member.param: The point from which to calculate the distance.
member.returns: The shortest distance from the point to the surface of the bounding box. If the point is inside the box, the method returns zero.
member.remarks: The method finds the closest point on the box's surface by clamping the given point to the box's bounds and returns the Euclidean distance between them. This ensures accurate distance calculations, even near corners or edges.

member.name: T:FixedMathSharp.BoundingSphere
member.summary: Represents a spherical bounding volume with fixed-point precision, optimized for fast, rotationally invariant spatial checks in 3D space.
member.remarks:
The BoundingSphere provides a simple yet effective way to represent the spatial extent of objects, especially when rotational invariance is required. Compared to BoundingBox, it offers faster intersection checks but is less precise in tightly fitting non-spherical objects.
Use Cases: - Ideal for broad-phase collision detection, proximity checks, and culling in physics engines and rendering pipelines. - Useful when fast, rotationally invariant checks are needed, such as detecting overlaps or distances between moving objects. - Suitable for encapsulating objects with roughly spherical shapes or objects that rotate frequently, where the bounding box may need constant updates.

member.summary: The center point of the sphere.

member.name: P:FixedMathSharp.BoundingSphere.Min
member.summary: Gets the coordinates of the minimum corner of the bounding box that contains the sphere.
member.remarks: The minimum corner is calculated by subtracting the radius from each component of the sphere's center. This property is useful for spatial queries and bounding box calculations.

member.name: P:FixedMathSharp.BoundingSphere.Max
member.summary: Gets the coordinates of the maximum corner of the bounding box that contains the sphere.
member.remarks: The maximum corner is calculated as the center of the sphere plus the radius in each dimension. This property is useful for spatial queries and bounding volume calculations.

member.name: T:FixedMathSharp.IBound
member.summary: Defines an axis-aligned bounding volume in three-dimensional space and provides methods for containment, intersection, and projection operations.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface represent a 3D region defined by minimum and maximum coordinates. The interface provides methods to determine whether a point is contained within the bounds, whether two bounds intersect, and to project a point onto the bounds. Typical use cases include spatial queries, collision detection, and geometric computations in 3D environments.

member.name: T:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath
member.summary: A static class that provides a variety of fixed-point math functions. Fixed-point numbers are represented as .

member.name: F:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.SHIFT_AMOUNT_I
member.summary: Represents the number of bits to shift for fixed-point representation.

member.name: F:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.MAX_SHIFTED_AMOUNT_UI
member.summary: Represents the maximum value that can be produced by left-shifting 1 by SHIFT_AMOUNT_I bits and subtracting 1.
member.remarks: This constant is typically used as a bitmask to extract or limit values to the range defined by SHIFT_AMOUNT_I. The value is always non-negative and fits within a 32-bit unsigned integer.

member.name: F:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.MASK_UL
member.summary: Represents a bitmask with all bits set except for the lowest SHIFT_AMOUNT_I bits.
member.remarks: This constant is typically used to isolate or clear the lower SHIFT_AMOUNT_I bits of an unsigned 64-bit value. The value of SHIFT_AMOUNT_I determines how many least significant bits are masked out.

member.name: F:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.MAX_VALUE_L
member.summary: Represents the largest possible value for a 64-bit fixed-point number.
member.remarks: Use this constant to perform comparisons or to initialize variables that require the maximum representable value for a 64-bit fixed-point type.

member.name: F:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.MIN_VALUE_L
member.summary: Represents the smallest possible value for a 64-bit fixed-point number.
member.remarks: Use this constant to check for underflow conditions or to initialize variables that require the minimum representable value for a 64-bit fixed-point type.

member.name: F:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.ONE_L
member.summary: Represents the value 1 shifted left by the number of bits specified by SHIFT_AMOUNT_I.

member.name: F:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.SCALE_FACTOR_F
member.summary: Represents the precomputed scale factor used for floating-point calculations.
member.remarks: This constant is intended only for converting fixed-point values to floating-point representations in performance-critical scenarios.

member.name: F:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.SCALE_FACTOR_D
member.summary: Represents the precomputed scale factor used for double-precision calculations.
member.remarks: This constant is intended only for converting fixed-point values to double-precision representations in performance-critical scenarios.

member.name: F:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.SCALE_FACTOR_M
member.summary: Represents the precomputed scale factor used for decimal calculations.
member.remarks: This constant is intended only for converting fixed-point values to decimal representations in performance-critical scenarios.

member.name: F:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.MIN_INCREMENT_L
member.summary: The smallest non-zero raw increment representable by Fixed64.

member.name: F:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.DEFAULT_TOLERANCE_L
member.summary: Default tolerance for fuzzy comparisons. Approximately 2^-24 (~5.96e-8) in value space.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.SmoothStep(FixedMathSharp.Fixed64,FixedMathSharp.Fixed64,FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)
member.summary: Performs a smooth step interpolation between two values.
member.remarks: The interpolation follows a cubic Hermite curve where the function starts at `a`, accelerates, and then decelerates towards `b`, ensuring smooth transitions.
member.param: The starting value.
The ending value.
A value between 0 and 1 that represents the interpolation factor.
member.returns: The interpolated value between `a` and `b`.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.CubicInterpolate(FixedMathSharp.Fixed64,FixedMathSharp.Fixed64,FixedMathSharp.Fixed64,FixedMathSharp.Fixed64,FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)
member.summary: Performs a cubic Hermite interpolation between two points, using specified tangents.
member.remarks: This method interpolates smoothly between `p0` and `p1` while considering the tangents `m0` and `m1`. It is useful for animation curves and smooth motion transitions.
member.param: The first point.
The second point.
The tangent (slope) at `p0`.
The tangent (slope) at `p1`.
A value between 0 and 1 that represents the interpolation factor.
member.returns: The interpolated value between `p0` and `p1`.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.LinearInterpolate(FixedMathSharp.Fixed64,FixedMathSharp.Fixed64,FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)
member.summary: Performs linear interpolation between two fixed-point values based on the interpolant t (0 greater or equal to `t` and less than or equal to 1).

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.MoveTowards(FixedMathSharp.Fixed64,FixedMathSharp.Fixed64,FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)
member.summary: Moves a value from 'from' to 'to' by a maximum step of 'maxAmount'. Ensures the value does not exceed 'to'.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.AddOverflowHelper(System.Int64,System.Int64,System.Boolean@)
member.summary: Adds two values and checks for overflow. If an overflow occurs during addition, the parameter is set to true.
member.param: The first operand to add.
The second operand to add.
A reference parameter that is set to true if an overflow is detected during the addition. The existing value of is preserved if already true.
member.returns: The sum of and .
member.remarks: Overflow is detected by checking for a change in the sign bit that indicates a wrap-around. Additionally, a special check is performed for adding and -1, as this is a known edge case for overflow.

member.name: F:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.Pow10Lookup
member.summary: Provides a lookup table of integer powers of 10 from 10^0 to 10^9.
member.remarks: This array can be used to efficiently retrieve the value of 10 raised to an integer exponent within the supported range, avoiding repeated calculations. The index corresponds to the exponent.

member.exception: The base was Fixed64.Zero, with a negative expFixed64.Onent
The base was negative, with a non-Fixed64.Zero expFixed64.Onent

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.Log2(FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)
member.summary: Returns the base-2 logarithm of a specified number. Provides at least 9 decimals of accuracy.
member.remarks: This implementation is based on Clay. S. Turner's fast binary logarithm algorithm (C. S. Turner, "A Fast Binary Logarithm Algorithm", IEEE Signal Processing Mag., pp. 124,140, Sep. 2010.)

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.Sqrt(FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.RadToDeg(FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)
member.summary: Converts a value in radians to degrees.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.DegToRad(FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.Sin(FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)
member.summary: Computes the sine of a given angle in radians using an optimized minimax polynomial approximation.
member.param: The angle in radians.
member.returns: The sine of the given angle, in fixed-point format.
member.remarks: - This function uses a Chebyshev-polynomial-based approximation to ensure high accuracy while maintaining performance in fixed-point arithmetic. - The coefficients have been carefully tuned to minimize fixed-point truncation errors. - The error is less than 1 ULP (unit in the last place) at key reference points, ensuring Sin(π/4) = 0.707106781192124 exactly within Fixed64 precision. - The function automatically normalizes input values to the range [-π, π] for stability.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.Cos(FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)
member.summary: Computes the cosine of a given angle in radians using a sine-based identity transformation.
member.param: The angle in radians.
member.returns: The cosine of the given angle, in fixed-point format.
member.remarks: - Instead of directly approximating cosine, this function derives cos(x) using the identity cos(x) = sin(x + π/2). This ensures maximum accuracy. - The underlying sine function is computed using a highly optimized minimax polynomial approximation. - By leveraging this transformation, cosine achieves the same precision guarantees as sine, including Cos(π/4) = 0.707106781192124 exactly within Fixed64 precision. - The function automatically normalizes input values to the range [-π, π] for stability.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.SinToCos(FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)
member.summary: Calculates the cosine value corresponding to a given sine value, assuming the angle is in the first or second quadrant.
member.remarks: This method returns the principal (non-negative) value of the cosine. If the input is outside the valid range for sine values, the result may not be meaningful.
member.param: The sine of the angle. Must be in the range [-1, 1].
member.returns: The cosine of the angle, computed as the positive square root of (1 - sin²).

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.Tan(FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)
member.summary: Returns the tangent of x.
member.remarks: This function is not well-tested. It may be wildly inaccurate.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.Asin(FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)
member.summary:
Returns the arc-sine of a fixed-point number x, which is the angle in radians whose sine is x, using a combination of a Taylor series expansion and trigonometric identities.
For values of x near ±1, the identity asin(x) = π/2 - acos(x) is used for stability. For values of x near 0, a Taylor series expansion is used.
member.param: The input value (sine) whose arcsine is to be computed. Should be in the range [-1, 1].
member.returns: The arc-sine of x in radians.
member.exception: Thrown if x is outside the domain [-1, 1].

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.Acos(FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)
member.summary: Returns the arccosine of the specified number x, calculated using a combination of the atan and sqrt functions.
member.param: The input value whose arccosine is to be computed. Should be in the range [-1, 1].
member.returns: The arccosine of x in radians.
member.exception: Thrown if x is outside the domain [-1, 1].

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.Atan(FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)
member.summary: Returns the arctangent of the specified number, using a more accurate approximation for larger values. This function has at least 7 decimals of accuracy.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.FixedMath.Atan2(FixedMathSharp.Fixed64,FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)
member.summary: Computes the angle whose tangent is the quotient of two specified numbers.
member.remarks: Uses a fixed-point arithmetic approximation for the arc tangent function, which is more efficient than using floating-point arithmetic, especially on systems where floating-point operations are expensive.
member.param: The y-coordinate of the point to which the angle is measured.
The x-coordinate of the point to which the angle is measured.
member.returns: An angle, θ, measured in radians, such that -π ≤ θ ≤ π, and tan(θ) = y / x, taking into account the quadrants of the inputs to determine the sign of the result.

member.name: T:FixedMathSharp.Fixed3x3
member.summary: Represents a 3x3 matrix used for linear transformations in 2D and 3D space, such as rotation, scaling, and shearing.
member.remarks:
A 3x3 matrix handles only linear transformations and is typically used when translation is not needed. It operates on directions, orientations, and vectors within a given space without affecting position. This matrix is more lightweight compared to a 4x4 matrix, making it ideal when translation and perspective are unnecessary.
Use Cases: - Rotating or scaling objects around the origin in 2D and 3D space. - Transforming vectors and normals (e.g., in lighting calculations). - Used in physics engines for inertia tensors or to represent local orientations. - Useful when optimizing transformations, as it omits the overhead of translation and perspective.

member.summary: Returns the identity matrix (no scaling, rotation, or translation).

member.name: P:FixedMathSharp.Fixed3x3.Item(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets or sets the matrix element at the specified index.
member.remarks: The mapping between indices and matrix elements is non-sequential. Ensure that the index corresponds to a valid matrix element.
member.param: The zero-based index of the matrix element to get or set. Valid values are 0, 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, and 10.
member.returns: The matrix element at the specified index.
member.exception: Thrown when the specified index is not one of the valid matrix element indices.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Fixed3x3.GetDeterminant
member.summary: Calculates the determinant of a 3x3 matrix.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Fixed3x3.InvertDiagonal
member.summary: Inverts the diagonal elements of the matrix.
member.remarks:
protects against the case where you would have an infinite value on the diagonal, which would cause problems in subsequent computations. If m00 or m22 are zero, handle that as a special case and manually set the inverse to zero, since for a theoretical object with no inertia along those axes, it would be impossible to impart a rotation in those directions
bear in mind that having a zero on the inertia tensor's diagonal isn't generally valid for real, 3-dimensional objects (unless they are "infinitely thin" along one axis), so if you end up with such a tensor, it's a sign that something else might be wrong in your setup.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Fixed3x3.Normalize(FixedMathSharp.Fixed3x3)
member.summary: Normalizes the basis vectors of a 3x3 matrix to ensure they are orthogonal and unit length.
member.remarks:
This method recalculates and normalizes the X, Y, and Z basis vectors of the matrix to avoid numerical drift that can occur after multiple transformations. It also ensures that the Z-axis is recomputed to maintain orthogonality by taking the cross-product of the normalized X and Y axes.
Use Cases: - Ensuring stability and correctness after repeated transformations involving rotation and scaling. - Useful in physics calculations where orthogonal matrices are required (e.g., inertia tensors or rotations).

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Fixed3x3.ResetScaleToIdentity(FixedMathSharp.Fixed3x3)

member.summary: Sets the global scale of an object using FixedMatrix3x3. Similar to SetGlobalScale for FixedMatrix4x4, but for a 3x3 matrix.
member.param: The transformation matrix (3x3) representing the object's global state.
The desired global scale represented as a Vector3d.
member.remarks: The method extracts the current global scale from the matrix and computes the new local scale by dividing the desired global scale by the current global scale. The new local scale is then applied to the matrix.

member.name: T:FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4
member.summary: Represents a 4x4 matrix used for transformations in 3D space, including translation, rotation, scaling, and perspective projection.
member.remarks:
A 4x4 matrix is the standard structure for 3D transformations because it can handle both linear transformations (rotation, scaling) and affine transformations (translation, shearing, and perspective projections). It is commonly used in graphics pipelines, game engines, and 3D rendering systems.
Use Cases: - Transforming objects in 3D space (position, orientation, and size). - Combining multiple transformations (e.g., model-view-projection matrices). - Applying translations, which require an extra dimension for homogeneous coordinates. - Useful in animation, physics engines, and 3D rendering for full transformation control.

member.returns: The matrix element at the specified index.
member.exception: Thrown when the specified index is less than 0 or greater than 15.

member.summary: Creates a rotation matrix from a quaternion.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4.CreateScale(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Creates a scale matrix from a 3-dimensional vector.
member.param: The vector representing the scale along each axis.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4.CreateTransform(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,FixedMathSharp.FixedQuaternion,FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Constructs a transformation matrix from translation, scale, and rotation. This method ensures that the rotation is properly normalized, applies the scale to the rotational basis, and sets the translation component separately.
member.remarks: - Uses a normalized rotation matrix to maintain numerical stability. - Applies non-uniform scaling to the rotation before setting translation. - Preferred when ensuring transformations remain mathematically correct. - If the rotation is already normalized and combined transformations are needed, consider using .
member.param: The translation vector.
The scale vector.
The rotation quaternion.
member.returns: A transformation matrix incorporating translation, rotation, and scale.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4.ScaleRotateTranslate(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,FixedMathSharp.FixedQuaternion,FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Constructs a transformation matrix from translation, rotation, and scale by multiplying separate matrices in the order: Scale * Rotation * Translation.
member.remarks: - This method directly multiplies the scale, rotation, and translation matrices. - Ensures that scale is applied first to preserve correct axis scaling. - Then rotation is applied so that rotation is not affected by non-uniform scaling. - Finally, translation moves the object to its correct world position.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4.TranslateRotateScale(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,FixedMathSharp.FixedQuaternion,FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Constructs a transformation matrix from translation, rotation, and scale by multiplying matrices in the order: Translation * Rotation * Scale (T * R * S).
member.remarks: - Use this method when transformations need to be applied **relative to an object's local origin**. - Example use cases include **animation systems**, **hierarchical transformations**, and **UI transformations**. - If you need to apply world-space transformations, use instead.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4.ExtractTranslation(FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4)
member.summary: Extracts the translation component from the 4x4 matrix.
member.param: The matrix from which to extract the translation.
member.returns: A Vector3d representing the translation component.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4.ExtractUp(FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4)
member.summary: Extracts the up direction from the 4x4 matrix.
member.remarks: This is the surface normal if the matrix represents ground orientation.
member.returns: A representing the up direction.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4.ExtractScale(FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4)
member.summary: Extracts the scaling factors from the matrix by calculating the magnitudes of the basis vectors (non-lossy).
member.returns: A Vector3d representing the precise scale along the X, Y, and Z axes.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4.ExtractLossyScale(FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4)
member.summary: Extracts the scaling factors from the matrix by returning the diagonal elements (lossy).
member.returns: A Vector3d representing the scale along X, Y, and Z axes (lossy).

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4.ExtractRotation(FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4)
member.summary: Extracts the rotation component from the 4x4 matrix by normalizing the rotation matrix.
member.param: The matrix from which to extract the rotation.
member.returns: A FixedQuaternion representing the rotation component.

member.summary: Decomposes a 4x4 matrix into its translation, scale, and rotation components.

The extracted scale component.
The extracted rotation component as a quaternion.
The extracted translation component.

member.summary: Sets the translation component of the 4x4 matrix.
member.param: The matrix to modify.
The new translation vector.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4.SetScale(FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4,FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Sets the scale component of the 4x4 matrix by assigning the provided scale vector to the matrix's diagonal elements.
member.param: The matrix to modify. Typically an identity or transformation matrix.
The new scale vector to apply along the X, Y, and Z axes.
member.remarks: Best used for applying scale to an identity matrix or resetting the scale on an existing matrix. For non-uniform scaling in combination with rotation, use .

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4.ApplyScaleToRotation(FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4,FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Applies non-uniform scaling to the 4x4 matrix by multiplying the scale vector with the rotation matrix's basis vectors.
member.param: The matrix to modify. Should already contain a valid rotation component.
The scale vector to apply along the X, Y, and Z axes.
member.remarks: Use this method when scaling is required in combination with an existing rotation, ensuring proper axis alignment.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4.ResetScaleToIdentity(FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4)
member.summary: Resets the scaling part of the matrix to identity (1,1,1).

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4.SetGlobalScale(FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4,FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Sets the global scale of an object using a 4x4 transformation matrix.
member.param: The transformation matrix representing the object's global state.
The desired global scale as a vector.
member.remarks: The method extracts the current global scale from the matrix and computes the new local scale by dividing the desired global scale by the current global scale. The new local scale is then applied to the matrix.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4.SetRotation(FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4,FixedMathSharp.FixedQuaternion)
member.summary: Replaces the rotation component of the 4x4 matrix using the provided quaternion, without affecting the translation component.
member.param: The matrix to modify. The rotation will replace the upper-left 3x3 portion of the matrix.
The quaternion representing the new rotation to apply.
member.remarks: This method preserves the matrix's translation component. For complete transformation updates, use .

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4.NormalizeRotationMatrix(FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4)
member.summary: Normalizes the rotation component of a 4x4 matrix by ensuring the basis vectors are orthogonal and unit length.
member.remarks:
This method recalculates the X, Y, and Z basis vectors from the upper-left 3x3 portion of the matrix, ensuring they are orthogonal and normalized. The remaining components of the matrix are reset to maintain a valid transformation structure.
Use Cases: - Ensuring the rotation component remains stable and accurate after multiple transformations. - Used in 3D transformations to prevent numerical drift from affecting the orientation over time. - Essential for cases where precise orientation is required, such as animations or physics simulations.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4.Invert(FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4,FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4@)
member.summary: Inverts the matrix if it is invertible (i.e., if the determinant is not zero).
member.remarks: To Invert a FixedMatrix4x4, we need to calculate the inverse for each element. This involves computing the cofactor for each element, which is the determinant of the submatrix when the row and column of that element are removed, multiplied by a sign based on the element's position. After computing all cofactors, the result is transposed to get the inverse matrix.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4.TransformPoint(FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4,FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Transforms a point from local space to world space using this transformation matrix.
member.remarks: This is the same as doing ` a * b`
member.param: The transformation matrix.
The local-space point.
member.returns: The transformed point in world space.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4.InverseTransformPoint(FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4,FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Transforms a point from world space into the local space of the matrix.
member.param: The transformation matrix.
The world-space point.
member.returns: The local-space point relative to the transformation matrix.

member.name: T:FixedMathSharp.Fixed64
member.summary: Represents a Q(64-SHIFT_AMOUNT).SHIFT_AMOUNT fixed-point number. Provides high precision for fixed-point arithmetic where SHIFT_AMOUNT bits are used for the fractional part and (64 - SHIFT_AMOUNT) bits for the integer part. The precision is determined by SHIFT_AMOUNT, which defines the resolution of fractional values.

member.name: F:FixedMathSharp.Fixed64.Two
member.summary: Represents the value 2 shifted left by the number of bits specified by SHIFT_AMOUNT_I.

member.name: F:FixedMathSharp.Fixed64.Three
member.summary: Represents the value 3 shifted left by the number of bits specified by SHIFT_AMOUNT_I.

member.name: F:FixedMathSharp.Fixed64.Half

member.name: F:FixedMathSharp.Fixed64.Quarter
member.summary: Represents the value 0.25 shifted left by the number of bits specified by SHIFT_AMOUNT_I.

member.name: F:FixedMathSharp.Fixed64.Eighth
member.summary: Represents the value 0.125 shifted left by the number of bits specified by SHIFT_AMOUNT_I.

member.name: F:FixedMathSharp.Fixed64.Zero
member.summary: Represents the value 0 as a fixed-point number.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.FixedQuaternion.GetMagnitude(FixedMathSharp.FixedQuaternion)
member.summary: Calculates the magnitude (or length) of the specified quaternion.
member.remarks: If rounding errors cause the computed magnitude to be slightly greater than 1 but within epsilon, the result is clamped to 1. This helps maintain numerical stability when working with normalized quaternions.
member.param: The quaternion for which to compute the magnitude.
member.returns: The magnitude of the quaternion as a Fixed64 value. Returns 0 if the quaternion is the zero quaternion.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.FixedQuaternion.FromEulerAngles(FixedMathSharp.Fixed64,FixedMathSharp.Fixed64,FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)
member.summary: Converts Euler angles (pitch, yaw, roll) to a quaternion and normalizes the result afterwards. Assumes the input angles are in radians.
member.remarks: The order of operations is YXZ or yaw-pitch-roll

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.FixedQuaternion.QuaternionLog(FixedMathSharp.FixedQuaternion)
member.summary: Computes the logarithm of a quaternion, which represents the rotational displacement. This is useful for interpolation and angular velocity calculations.
member.param: The quaternion to compute the logarithm of.
member.returns: A Vector3d representing the logarithm of the quaternion (axis-angle representation).
member.remarks: The logarithm of a unit quaternion is given by: log(q) = (θ * v̂), where: - θ = 2 * acos(w) is the rotation angle. - v̂ = (x, y, z) / ||(x, y, z)|| is the unit vector representing the axis of rotation. If the quaternion is close to identity, the function returns a zero vector to avoid numerical instability.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.FixedQuaternion.ToAngularVelocity(FixedMathSharp.FixedQuaternion,FixedMathSharp.FixedQuaternion,FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)
member.summary: Computes the angular velocity required to move from `previousRotation` to `currentRotation` over a given time step.
member.param: The current orientation as a quaternion.
The previous orientation as a quaternion.
The time step over which the rotation occurs.
member.returns: A Vector3d representing the angular velocity (in radians per second).
member.remarks: This function calculates the change in rotation over `deltaTime` and converts it into angular velocity. - First, it computes the relative rotation: `rotationDelta = currentRotation * previousRotation.Inverse()`. - Then, it applies `QuaternionLog(rotationDelta)` to extract the axis-angle representation. - Finally, it divides by `deltaTime` to compute the angular velocity.

member.summary: Performs a simple linear interpolation between the components of the input quaternions

member.returns: A new FixedQuaternion whose components are the sums of the corresponding components of q1 and q2.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.FixedQuaternion.ToEulerAngles
member.summary:
Converts this quaternion to Euler angles in degrees. Returns angles as (pitch, yaw, roll), where: pitch = rotation around X yaw = rotation around Y roll = rotation around Z
The extraction matches FromEulerAngles(), which composes rotations in YXZ order: q = qy * qx * qz

member.name: T:FixedMathSharp.Vector2d
member.summary: Represents a 2D vector with fixed-point precision, offering a range of mathematical operations and transformations such as rotation, scaling, reflection, and interpolation.
member.remarks:
The Vector2d struct is designed for applications that require precise numerical operations, such as games, simulations, or physics engines. It provides methods for common vector operations like addition, subtraction, dot product, cross product, distance calculations, and rotation.
Use Cases: - Modeling 2D positions, directions, and velocities in fixed-point math environments. - Performing vector transformations, including rotations and reflections. - Handling interpolation and distance calculations in physics or simulation systems. - Useful for fixed-point math scenarios where floating-point precision is insufficient or not desired.

member.remarks: This comparison uses the squared magnitude of each vector, which avoids the computational cost of calculating the actual magnitude. Use this method when only relative vector lengths are important.

member.name: T:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d
member.summary: Represents a 3D vector with fixed-point precision, supporting a wide range of vector operations such as rotation, scaling, interpolation, and projection.
member.remarks:
The Vector3d struct is designed for high-precision applications in 3D space, including games, simulations, and physics engines. It offers essential operations like addition, subtraction, dot product, cross product, distance calculation, and normalization.
Use Cases: - Modeling 3D positions, directions, and velocities with fixed-point precision. - Performing vector transformations, including rotations using quaternions. - Calculating distances, angles, projections, and interpolation between vectors. - Essential for fixed-point math scenarios where floating-point precision isn't suitable.

member.name: P:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.RightHandNormal
member.summary: Provides a rotated version of the current vector, where rotation is a 90 degrees rotation around the Y axis in the counter-clockwise direction.
member.remarks: These operations rotate the vector 90 degrees around the Y-axis. Note that the positive direction of rotation is defined by the right-hand rule: If your right hand's thumb points in the positive Y direction, then your fingers curl in the positive direction of rotation.

member.name: P:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.LeftHandNormal

member.remarks: This is commonly used to determine the direction an object is facing in 3D space, where 'x' represents the yaw (horizontal rotation) and 'y' represents the pitch (vertical rotation).

member.summary: Returns a new vector with components whose absolute values are less than the specified threshold set to zero.
member.remarks: This method is useful for eliminating insignificant floating-point errors by zeroing out very small vector components. The default threshold is suitable for most cases where near-zero values are considered noise.
member.param: The minimum absolute value a component must have to be retained. If null, a default epsilon value is used.
member.returns: A new Vector3d instance with small components snapped to zero based on the specified threshold.

member.summary: Linearly interpolates between two points.

End value, returned when t = 1.
Value used to interpolate between a and b.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.UnclampedLerp(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)
member.summary: Linearly interpolates between two vectors without clamping the interpolation factor between 0 and 1.
member.param: The start vector.
The end vector.
The interpolation factor. Values outside the range [0, 1] will cause the interpolation to go beyond the start or end points.
member.returns: The interpolated vector.
member.remarks: Unlike traditional Lerp, this function allows interpolation factors greater than 1 or less than 0, which means the resulting vector can extend beyond the endpoints.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.Slerp(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)
member.summary: Spherically interpolates between two vectors, moving along the shortest arc on a unit sphere.
member.param: The starting vector.
The ending vector.
A value between 0 and 1 that represents the interpolation amount. 0 returns the start vector, and 1 returns the end vector.
member.returns: The interpolated vector between the two input vectors.
member.remarks: Slerp is used to interpolate between two unit vectors on a sphere, providing smooth rotation. It can be more computationally expensive than linear interpolation (Lerp) but results in smoother, arc-like motion.

The second vector.

The second vector.
member.returns: The minimized vector.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.op_Addition(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Adds two Vector3d instances component-wise.
member.param: The first vector to add.
The second vector to add.
member.returns: A new Vector3d whose components are the sum of the corresponding components of v1 and v2.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.op_Addition(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)
member.summary: Adds a scalar value to each component of the specified vector and returns the resulting vector.
member.param: The vector to which the scalar value will be added.
The scalar value to add to each component of the vector.
member.returns: A new Vector3d whose components are the sum of the corresponding components of the input vector and the scalar value.

member.summary: Adds a Vector3d instance to a tuple representing x, y, and z components and returns the resulting vector.
member.param: The first vector to add.
A tuple containing the x, y, and z values to add to the vector.
member.returns: A new Vector3d that is the sum of the original vector and the specified tuple components.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.op_Addition(System.ValueTuple{System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32},FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)

The Vector3d instance to which the tuple components are added.
member.returns: A new Vector3d representing the sum of the original vector and the specified tuple components.

member.summary: Subtracts the components of one Vector3d from another and returns the resulting vector.
member.param: The vector to subtract from.
The vector to subtract.
member.returns: A Vector3d whose components are the result of subtracting the corresponding components of v2 from v1.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.op_Subtraction(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)
member.summary: Subtracts the specified scalar value from each component of the given vector.
member.param: The vector from which to subtract the scalar value from each component.
The scalar value to subtract from each component of the vector.
member.returns: A new Vector3d whose components are the result of subtracting the scalar value from the corresponding components of the input vector.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.op_Subtraction(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,System.ValueTuple{System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32})
member.summary: Subtracts the specified tuple from the given vector and returns the resulting vector.
member.param: The vector from which to subtract the tuple values.
A tuple containing the x, y, and z values to subtract from the vector.
member.returns: A new Vector3d representing the result of subtracting the tuple values from the original vector.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.op_Subtraction(System.ValueTuple{System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32},FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Subtracts the components of a specified Vector3d from the corresponding components of a 3-tuple of integers and returns the resulting Vector3d.
member.remarks: This operator enables direct subtraction between a tuple of three integers and a Vector3d, returning a new Vector3d instance.
member.param: A tuple containing the x, y, and z components to subtract from.
The Vector3d whose components are subtracted from the corresponding components of the tuple.
member.returns: A Vector3d whose components are the result of subtracting the components of v2 from the corresponding components of v1.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.op_UnaryNegation(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Negates each component of the specified vector.
member.param: The vector whose components are to be negated.
member.returns: A new vector whose components are the negated values of the input vector.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.op_Multiply(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)
member.summary: Multiplies the specified vector by a scalar value.
member.param: The vector to be scaled.
The scalar value by which to multiply each component of the vector.
member.returns: A new vector whose components are the products of the corresponding components of the input vector and the scalar value.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.op_Multiply(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,System.Int32)
member.summary: Multiplies each component of the specified vector by the given scalar value.
member.param: The vector whose components are to be multiplied.
The scalar value by which to multiply each component of the vector.
member.returns: A new Vector3d whose components are the products of the corresponding components of the input vector and the scalar value.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.op_Multiply(FixedMathSharp.Fixed3x3,FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Multiplies a 3x3 matrix by a 3-dimensional vector and returns the resulting vector.
member.remarks: This operation applies the linear transformation represented by the matrix to the vector. The multiplication is performed using standard matrix-vector multiplication rules.
member.param: The 3x3 matrix to multiply.
The 3-dimensional vector to be transformed by the matrix.
member.returns: A new Vector3d that is the result of multiplying the specified matrix by the specified vector.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.op_Multiply(FixedMathSharp.Fixed4x4,FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Transforms the specified 3D vector by the given 4x4 matrix using homogeneous coordinates.
member.remarks: If the matrix is affine, the transformation is performed without perspective division. For non-affine matrices, the result is divided by the computed w component to account for perspective transformations. If the computed w component is zero, it is treated as one to avoid division by zero.
member.param: The 4x4 matrix to apply to the vector. Must represent a valid transformation.
The 3D vector to be transformed.
member.returns: A new Vector3d representing the transformed vector after applying the matrix.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.op_Multiply(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Multiplies the corresponding components of two vectors and returns the resulting vector.
member.remarks: This operation performs component-wise multiplication, not a dot or cross product. Each component of the result is calculated as the product of the corresponding components of the input vectors.
member.param: The first vector to multiply.
The second vector to multiply.
member.returns: A new Vector3d whose components are the products of the corresponding components of the input vectors.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.op_Division(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,FixedMathSharp.Fixed64)
member.summary: Divides each component of a vector by a specified scalar value.
member.remarks: If the scalar value is zero, the result is a zero vector to avoid division by zero.
member.param: The vector whose components are to be divided.
The scalar value by which to divide each component of the vector.
member.returns: A new vector whose components are the result of dividing the corresponding components of the input vector by the specified scalar. Returns a zero vector if the scalar is zero.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.op_Division(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Divides each component of one vector by the corresponding component of another vector.
member.remarks: Division by zero for any component in v2 results in a zero value for the corresponding component in the result vector.
member.param: The vector whose components are to be divided (the dividend).
The vector whose components are used as divisors.
member.returns: A new Vector3d whose components are the result of dividing the corresponding components of v1 by v2. If a component of v2 is zero, the corresponding result component is set to zero.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.op_Division(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,System.Int32)
member.summary: Divides each component of a vector by the specified integer value.
member.param: The vector whose components are to be divided.
The integer divisor. If zero, the result is a zero vector.
member.returns: A new vector whose components are the result of dividing each component of the input vector by the specified divisor. Returns a zero vector if the divisor is zero.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.op_Multiply(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,FixedMathSharp.FixedQuaternion)
member.summary: Rotates the specified 3D point by the given quaternion.
member.remarks: This operator applies the rotation to the point as if performing a geometric transformation in 3D space.
member.param: The 3D point to be rotated.
The quaternion representing the rotation to apply.
member.returns: A new Vector3d representing the rotated point.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.op_Equality(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Determines whether two Vector3d instances are equal.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.op_Inequality(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Determines whether two Vector3d instances are not equal.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.op_GreaterThan(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Determines whether each component of the left vector is greater than the corresponding component of the right vector.
member.param: The first vector to compare.
The second vector to compare.
member.returns: true if the x, y, and z components of left are all greater than those of right; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.op_LessThan(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Determines whether each component of the first Vector3d is less than the corresponding component of the second Vector3d.
member.remarks: This operator performs a component-wise comparison. All components of left must be less than the corresponding components of right for the result to be true.
member.param: The first Vector3d to compare.
The second Vector3d to compare.
member.returns: true if the x, y, and z components of left are all less than those of right; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.op_GreaterThanOrEqual(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Determines whether each component of the left Vector3d is greater than or equal to the corresponding component of the right Vector3d.
member.param: The first Vector3d to compare.
The second Vector3d to compare.
member.returns: true if the x, y, and z components of left are each greater than or equal to those of right; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.op_LessThanOrEqual(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d,FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Determines whether each component of the first Vector3d is less than or equal to the corresponding component of the second Vector3d.
member.param: The first Vector3d to compare.
The second Vector3d to compare.
member.returns: true if the x, y, and z components of left are each less than or equal to the corresponding components of right; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.ToString
member.summary: Returns a string that represents the current object in the format "(x, y, z)".
member.returns: A string representation of the object, displaying the x, y, and z values in a formatted tuple.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.ToVector2d
member.summary: Converts this to a , dropping the Y component (height) of this vector in the resulting vector.
member.returns: A new where (X, Z) from this become (X, Y) in the resulting vector.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.Deconstruct(System.Single@,System.Single@,System.Single@)
member.summary: Deconstructs the vector into its X, Y, and Z components as single-precision floating-point values.
member.param: When this method returns, contains the X component of the vector as a single-precision floating-point value.
When this method returns, contains the Y component of the vector as a single-precision floating-point value.
When this method returns, contains the Z component of the vector as a single-precision floating-point value.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.Deconstruct(System.Int32@,System.Int32@,System.Int32@)
member.summary: Deconstructs the current instance into its component integer values.
member.remarks: This method enables deconstruction syntax, allowing the instance to be unpacked into three integer variables representing its components.
member.param: When this method returns, contains the rounded integer value of the X component.
When this method returns, contains the rounded integer value of the Y component.
When this method returns, contains the rounded integer value of the Z component.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.ToDegrees(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Converts each component of the vector from radians to degrees.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.ToRadians(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Converts each component of the vector from degrees to radians.
member.param: The vector with components in degrees.
member.returns: A new vector with components converted to radians.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Vector3d.CompareTo(FixedMathSharp.Vector3d)
member.summary: Compares the current Vector3d instance with another Vector3d based on their squared magnitudes.
member.remarks: This comparison uses the squared magnitude of each vector, which avoids the computational cost of calculating the actual magnitude. Use this method when only relative vector lengths are important.
member.param: The Vector3d instance to compare with the current instance.
member.returns: A value less than zero if this instance is less than ; zero if this instance is equal to ; or a value greater than zero if this instance is greater than , as determined by their squared magnitudes.

member.name: T:FixedMathSharp.Utility.DeterministicRandom
member.summary: Fast, seedable, deterministic RNG suitable for lockstep sims and map gen. Uses xoroshiro128++ with splitmix64 seeding. No allocations, no time/GUID.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Utility.DeterministicRandom.#ctor(System.UInt64)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the DeterministicRandom class using the specified seed value.
member.remarks: This constructor expands the provided seed into the internal state required for deterministic random number generation. The generated sequence is fully determined by the seed value.
member.param: The initial seed value used to generate the internal state. Using the same seed will produce the same sequence of random numbers.

member.name: M:FixedMathSharp.Utility.DeterministicRandom.FromWorldFeature(System.UInt64,System.UInt64,System.UInt64)
member.summary: Create a stream deterministically Derived from (worldSeed, featureKey[,index]).
